reasoning: With the knowledge cutoff at November 9, 2025, there are only about 7 weeks left before the resolution date of December 31, 2025. No $100M+ movie released or scheduled to be released by then is acknowledged to have been written or significantly assisted by AI. The 2023 WGA strike resolved with strict contractual prohibitions against AI being credited as a writer or used to generate 'literary material', and current U.S. copyright law offers no protection for AI-generated scripts. A $100 million movie requires massive investment and union compliance, meaning no major studio would risk its IP or breach WGA rules. Known AI film projects (like 'The Last Screenwriter') are small, non-blockbuster experimental works. Given the lack of any existing candidates and the long production timelines of blockbusters, it is virtually impossible for one to materialize and meet the criteria before the end of 2025.
